user.title: Set up Declarative State Sensor
user.description: Enter a unique name for this sensor. You will configure states and other options in the next steps.

data_description.name: Unique name for this sensor (used as entity ID)

done.title: Sensor Created
done.description: Setup complete. The sensor is now active.

error.invalid_time_format: Invalid time format. Use HH:MM, HH, *-MM-DDThh:mm, or a Jinja2 template.
error.already_configured: A sensor with this name is already configured.

init.title: Configure Declarative State
init.description: Choose what you want to configure:

data_description.lookahead: Number of future state transitions to pre-calculate and expose as attributes (0 = off, max 10)
data_description.error_handling: What to do if a state rule fails to evaluate: ignore the error and fall through, or mark the sensor unavailable
data_description.no_sensor: When enabled, no sensor entity is created in HA. Useful when you only need time-based control of a target entity and do not want a visible sensor.

target_settings.title: Target
target_settings.description: Optionally control entities based on the calculated state. Supports individual entities, areas, devices, and labels. Clear the target to disable.

data_description.target: The entities (or area/device/label) to control whenever the sensor state changes
data_description.sync: If enabled, the action is re-applied automatically whenever a target entity drifts from its expected state (e.g. if changed externally)
data_description.sync_attribute: Attribute to watch for drift instead of the entity state. Leave empty to compare the entity state directly.

generic_action.title: Generic Action
generic_action.description:
The fallback action called for any state that does not have a per-state action configured. The action editor supports templates — use {{ state }} anywhere in the service data to pass the current state value.

Note: Any entity or target selected in the action editor is ignored — the target configured in "Target Entity" is always used.

data_description.action: Action to call (e.g. light.turn_on). Service data fields support Jinja2 templates — e.g. brightness_pct: {{ state | int }}.
data_description.expected_state: What the target entity should report after the action is applied. Leave empty to use the sensor state value as the expected state.

per_state_actions.title: Per-state Actions
per_state_actions.description:
Configure a specific action for each state value. States without an action configured here will fall back to the Default Action.

Each section corresponds to one state value.

Note: Any entity or target selected in the action editor is ignored — the target configured in "Target Entity" is always used.

data_description.action: Action to call when the sensor enters this state. Leave empty to use the Default Action.
data_description.expected_state: What the target entity should report after this action. Leave empty to use the state value as the expected state.

manage_states.title: Manage States
manage_states.description:
Currently configured states: {count}

Last state in the list has the highest priority.

add_state.title: Add New State
add_state.description: Add a new state rule. It will be added at the end (highest priority).

data_description.state: The value this sensor will report when this rule is active (e.g. 'on', 'off', '200')
data_description.start: When this state starts. Formats: HH:MM, *-MM-DDThh:mm, or Jinja2 template. Leave empty for 'always'.
data_description.end: When this state ends. Same formats as start. Leave empty for 'always'.
data_description.conditions: HA conditions that must all be true for this state to be active. Leave empty for no conditions.

edit_state_select.title: Select State to Edit
edit_state_select.description: Choose which state you want to edit:

data_description.state: The value this sensor will report when this rule is active
data_description.start: When this state starts. Leave empty for 'always'.
data_description.end: When this state ends. Leave empty for 'always'.
data_description.conditions: HA conditions that must all be true for this state to be active. Leave empty for no conditions.

remove_state_select.title: Select State to Remove
remove_state_select.description: Choose which state you want to remove:

confirm_remove.title: Confirm Removal
confirm_remove.description:
Are you sure you want to remove this state?

{state}

done.title: Configuration Complete
done.description: Your changes have been saved. The integration will reload automatically.

error.invalid_time_format: Invalid time format. Use HH:MM, HH, *-MM-DDThh:mm, or a Jinja2 template.
error.removal_not_confirmed: You must check the confirmation box to remove the state.
